blueprintPromptRules: Zero resultant force means rest or constant velocity.
Constant velocity means zero acceleration and zero resultant force.
Resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.
For force to be directly proportional to acceleration, mass must be constant.
Do not say force is inversely proportional to mass.
Friction acts between surfaces in contact and opposes relative motion or the tendency of relative motion.
Do not say friction always causes deceleration.
Air resistance is drag that opposes motion through air.
Do not say air resistance always causes deceleration.
Normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface.
Do not say normal contact force causes an object to remain at rest.
assessmentPromptRules: If explaining how mass affects acceleration, state that for the same resultant force, a larger mass gives a smaller acceleration.
Do not say friction always causes deceleration.
For friction questions, say that friction opposes relative motion or the tendency of relative motion.
Do not say air resistance always causes deceleration.
Do not say friction is zero just because velocity is constant.
Normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface.
Describe gravity or weight as acting vertically downwards.
Do not say normal contact force supports weight unless the object is at rest on a horizontal surface.

blueprintPromptRules: Treat horizontal and vertical motion independently.
When air resistance is ignored, horizontal acceleration is zero.
When air resistance is ignored, horizontal velocity remains constant.
Vertical acceleration is constant and directed vertically downwards.
At the highest point, vertical velocity is zero but horizontal velocity is not zero.
Do not say total velocity is zero at the highest point.
Do not say gravity acts horizontally.
Do not say acceleration is zero at the highest point.
The only force acting on an ideal projectile after launch is its weight.
assessmentPromptRules: Keep horizontal and vertical velocity components clearly distinguished.
Do not treat zero vertical velocity at the highest point as zero resultant velocity.
State that gravity acts vertically downwards.
Do not say horizontal velocity changes when air resistance is ignored.
Do not say the projectile has zero acceleration at the highest point.
Do not treat a condition already supplied in the prompt as an earned mark.
For multi-mark explanations, require separate physical ideas.
